title: saussurea sclerolepis var. parasclerolepis
intro: Saussurea sclerolepis var. parasclerolepis, also known as the Sikkim saussurea, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. The herbal preparation of Saussurea sclerolepis var. parasclerolepis is typically in the form of a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat various health issues.
